summary: Package executes AES-decrypted opaque payload at import time
details: dist/index.js ships a ~6 KB base64 blob that is AES-256-CBC-decrypted at module load using a secret read from environment variable VITE_DOT_UTILS_AES_SECRET, then dynamically imported as a data:text/javascript;base64 URL (`const moduleUrl = toDataUrl(decryptedSource); return await import(moduleUrl);` invoked from top-level `const loadedPromise = loadDecryptedModule();`). The package's only declared export, `uninstall`, is a thin pass-through to a function inside that hidden module — there is no library functionality outside the encrypted payload. Because the plaintext of the executed code is not auditable in the published artifact, anyone holding the AES key (the author, or any attacker who obtains it) controls arbitrary code that runs in every consumer on require. Reinforcing signals: placeholder metadata (`author: "Your Name"`), a vague generic description, and a README that documents no API — only build/publish steps. This matches the eval-of-opaque-blob attack shape; the entire package is a delivery vehicle for non-auditable code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
